Elixir in 100 seconds

Nov 27, 2022

Last modified at: November 27 2022

Hi all,

After watch a video on YouTube I want share a transcript of shown elements.

The Elixir is dynamic, general purpose programming language designed to build scalable concurrent applications with a beautiful functional syntax.

Here is the direct URL to video: Elixir in 100 Seconds

The Fireship present the basics Elixir programming language in very good way.

Preface

“Elixir” |> String.upcase() |> String.split()”

Created by Jose Valim in 2012.

Build on Erlang BEAM Virtual Machine to use for Open Telecom Platform.

Dynamic typed with minimal Ruby inspired syntax with static typed like Haskell.

Compiled to BEAM bytecode to deliver better performance and scalability.

Companies using Elixir

Used by companies like:

  • Discord (concarency)
  • Motorola (fault tolerancy)
  • Pinterest (Realtime)
  • Phoenix (Framework)

Code example

The Elixir file has exs file extention.

  • Example 1

hello = “Hi Mom!”

IO.puts hello <– Clothing optional

IO.puts :hi_mom <– Atom

add_five = fn n -> n + 5 end <– Anonymous function

Part Four: Real world Elixir

  • Introduction to Mix

It is everything that I have for today.
Everyone is a blacksmith of own fate.
Mateusz.
